methodsAttempted.query: site:illinois.edu generative AI guidance policy students faculty data privacy
methodsAttempted.notes: Searched official Illinois domains and followed Enterprise GenAI, CITL, and Graduate College official pages.
Reviewed CITL teaching implications page linked from official Illinois AI resources.

artifacts.summary: Official Illinois AI guidance was found on Enterprise GenAI, CITL, and Graduate College pages. Unit-level or hub-only pages were rejected where they were too broad or not university-wide policy evidence.

artifacts.claimText: Illinois Enterprise GenAI guidance frames AI as a collaborator for efficiency and creativity rather than a replacement for human insight.

artifacts.claimText: Illinois Enterprise GenAI guidance for service providers includes security controls, audits, MFA, and data privacy compliance for AI systems and sensitive data.

citation.citationTitle: Best Practice: Security, Privacy, and Accessibility | Enterprise GenAI | Office of the Chief Information Officer | Illinois
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Best Practice: Security, Privacy, and Accessibility | Enterprise GenAI | Office of the Chief Information Officer | Illinois
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Service providers should implement robust security measures to protect against unauthorized access and data breaches; conduct regular security audits and vulnerability assessments; enforce the use of multi-factor authentication for accessing AI systems and sensitive data; and ensure compliance with all relevant data privacy laws and regulations, such as GDPR, CCPA, FERPA and HIPAA.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Teaching with AI | Center for Innovation in Teaching & Learning
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Teaching with AI | Center for Innovation in Teaching & Learning
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Faculty should clearly define the boundaries for using AI in student work to maintain academic integrity and discourage plagiarism. Students should be educated about the ethical use of AI tools and reminded that text and ideas generated by AI must be cited to avoid plagiarism.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Illinois CITL teaching guidance says faculty should not require students to register for AI platforms using their official university email address and should safeguard student data under FERPA.

citation.citationTitle: Teaching with AI | Center for Innovation in Teaching & Learning
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Teaching with AI | Center for Innovation in Teaching & Learning
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Faculty should never require students to register for any platform using their official university email address. It is the faculty members' responsibility to safeguard student data following all relevant regulations covered by FERPA.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The Illinois Graduate College states it does not have a policy on permissibility of generative AI in doctoral milestones, and encourages programs and committees to communicate their expectations.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI Guidance | Graduate College
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The Graduate College does not have a policy regarding the permissibility of the use of generative AI in doctoral milestones. However, we encourage discussion within programs and individual committees about their expectations for if and how generative AI can be used in exams and theses.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Guidance on the Use of Generative AI in Graduate Exams, Theses, and Dissertations

artifacts.title: University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign staged AI policy source review
artifacts.summary: Staged machine-reviewed claim candidates were created from official Illinois Enterprise GenAI, CITL, and Graduate College sources. Claims are limited to guidance on AI as a supportive tool, transparency/citation, data handling, security/privacy, teaching boundaries, and graduate milestone expectations.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Illinois artifacts were completed locally after the isolated Codex CLI worker hit a usage-limit retry blocker.
